authors_text: Qinglai Wei,Diancheng Chen,Beiming Yuan
title: Multi-Viewpoint and Multi-Evaluation with Felicitous Inductive Bias Boost Machine Abstract Reasoning Ability
title_zh: 多视角多评价恰到好处的归纳偏差提升机器抽象推理能力
abstract: Great endeavors have been made to study AI's ability in abstract reasoning, along with which different versions of RAVEN's progressive matrices (RPM) are proposed as benchmarks. Previous works give inkling that without sophisticated design or extra meta-data containing semantic information, neural networks may still be indecisive in making decisions regarding RPM problems, after relentless training. Evidenced by thorough experiments and ablation studies, we showcase that end-to-end neural networks embodied with felicitous inductive bias, intentionally design or serendipitously match, can solve RPM problems elegantly, without the augment of any extra meta-data or preferences of any specific backbone. Our work also reveals that multi-viewpoint with multi-evaluation is a key learning strategy for successful reasoning. Finally, potential explanations for the failure of connectionist models in generalization are provided. We hope that these results will serve as inspections of AI's ability beyond perception and toward abstract reasoning. Source code can be found in https://github.com/QinglaiWeiCASIA/RavenSolver.
summary: Great endeavors have been made to study AI's ability in abstract reasoning, along with which different versions of RAVEN's progressive matrices (RPM) are proposed as benchmarks. Previous works give inkling that without sophisticated design or extra meta-data containing semantic information, neural networks may still be indecisive in making decisions regarding RPM problems, after relentless training. Evidenced by thorough experiments and ablation studies, we showcase that end-to-end neural networks embodied with felicitous inductive bias, intentionally design or serendipitously match, can solve RPM problems elegantly, without the augment of any extra meta-data or preferences of any specific backbone. Our work also reveals that multi-viewpoint with multi-evaluation is a key learning strategy for successful reasoning. Finally, potential explanations for the failure of connectionist models in generalization are provided. We hope that these results will serve as inspections of AI's ability beyond perception and toward abstract reasoning. Source code can be found in https://github.com/QinglaiWeiCASIA/RavenSolver.
abstract_zh: 人们为研究人工智能的抽象推理能力付出了巨大的努力，并提出了不同版本的 RAVEN 渐进矩阵（RPM）作为基准。之前的研究表明，如果没有复杂的设计或包含语义信息的额外元数据，神经网络在经过不懈的训练后，在做出有关 RPM 问题的决策时可能仍然犹豫不决。通过彻底的实验和消融研究证明，我们展示了端到端神经网络体现了适当的归纳偏差、有意设计或偶然匹配，可以优雅地解决 RPM 问题，而无需增加任何额外的元数据或任何特定主干的偏好。我们的工作还表明，多观点和多评估是成功推理的关键学习策略。最后，提供了联结主义模型在泛化方面失败的潜在解释。我们希望这些结果能够检验人工智能超越感知、走向抽象推理的能力。源代码可以在 https://github.com/QinglaiWeiCASIA/RavenSolver 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过多视角与多评估结合恰当归纳偏置的方法，使端到端神经网络在无需额外元数据的情况下优雅解决RAVEN渐进矩阵（RPM）问题，提升机器抽象推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络在缺乏复杂设计或语义元数据时，对RPM问题决策存在不确定性，需探索无需额外信息的归纳偏置设计以实现有效推理。
AI.method: 设计具有恰当归纳偏置的端到端神经网络，结合多视角分析与多评估策略，通过彻底实验与消融研究验证方法有效性，揭示连接主义模型泛化失败的潜在原因。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法无需额外元数据或特定主干网络偏好即可高效解决RPM问题，同时为连接主义模型在抽象推理中的局限性提供解释。
AI.conclusion: 本工作证明了多视角多评估策略与归纳偏置结合对抽象推理的关键作用，为超越感知的AI能力研究提供了新视角与实践参考。

authors_text: Yeongmin Kim,Huiwon Jang,DongKeon Lee,Ho-Jin Choi
title: AltUB: Alternating Training Method to Update Base Distribution of Normalizing Flow for Anomaly Detection
title_zh: AltUB：交替训练方法更新归一化流的基分布以进行异常检测
abstract: Unsupervised anomaly detection is coming into the spotlight these days in various practical domains due to the limited amount of anomaly data. One of the major approaches for it is a normalizing flow which pursues the invertible transformation of a complex distribution as images into an easy distribution as N(0, I). In fact, algorithms based on normalizing flow like FastFlow and CFLOW-AD establish state-of-the-art performance on unsupervised anomaly detection tasks. Nevertheless, we investigate these algorithms convert normal images into not N(0, I) as their destination, but an arbitrary normal distribution. Moreover, their performances are often unstable, which is highly critical for unsupervised tasks because data for validation are not provided. To break through these observations, we propose a simple solution AltUB which introduces alternating training to update the base distribution of normalizing flow for anomaly detection. AltUB effectively improves the stability of performance of normalizing flow. Furthermore, our method achieves the new state-of-the-art performance of the anomaly segmentation task on the MVTec AD dataset with 98.8% AUROC.
summary: Unsupervised anomaly detection is coming into the spotlight these days in various practical domains due to the limited amount of anomaly data. One of the major approaches for it is a normalizing flow which pursues the invertible transformation of a complex distribution as images into an easy distribution as N(0, I). In fact, algorithms based on normalizing flow like FastFlow and CFLOW-AD establish state-of-the-art performance on unsupervised anomaly detection tasks. Nevertheless, we investigate these algorithms convert normal images into not N(0, I) as their destination, but an arbitrary normal distribution. Moreover, their performances are often unstable, which is highly critical for unsupervised tasks because data for validation are not provided. To break through these observations, we propose a simple solution AltUB which introduces alternating training to update the base distribution of normalizing flow for anomaly detection. AltUB effectively improves the stability of performance of normalizing flow. Furthermore, our method achieves the new state-of-the-art performance of the anomaly segmentation task on the MVTec AD dataset with 98.8% AUROC.
abstract_zh: 由于异常数据量有限，无监督异常检测如今在各个实际领域中受到关注。它的主要方法之一是归一化流，它追求将图像形式的复杂分布转换为 N(0, I) 形式的简单分布。事实上，基于归一化流的算法（如 FastFlow 和 CFLOW-AD）在无监督异常检测任务上建立了最先进的性能。尽管如此，我们研究这些算法将正态图像转换为任意正态分布，而不是 N(0, I) 作为其目的地。此外，它们的性能通常不稳定，这对于无监督任务非常关键，因为没有提供验证数据。为了突破这些观察结果，我们提出了一个简单的解决方案 AltUB，它引入交替训练来更新异常检测的归一化流的基础分布。 AltUB有效提高了正火流性能的稳定性。此外，我们的方法在 MVTec AD 数据集上实现了异常分割任务的最新性能，AUROC 为 98.8%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AltUB方法，通过交替训练更新归一化流的基础分布，以提升无监督异常检测的稳定性与性能。
AI.motivation: 现有归一化流方法在转换正常图像时未严格遵循标准正态分布，且性能不稳定，缺乏验证数据导致难以优化。
AI.method: 设计交替训练机制，动态更新归一化流的基分布参数，使模型更适应真实数据分布并增强鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在MVTec AD数据集的异常分割任务中达到98.8% AUROC，刷新了当前最优性能。
AI.conclusion: AltUB通过改进基分布更新策略，有效解决了归一化流在无监督异常检测中的稳定性与性能瓶颈问题。

authors_text: Alexandre Duval,Anita Paas,Abdalwhab Abdalwhab,David St-Onge
title: The eyes and hearts of UAV pilots: observations of physiological responses in real-life scenarios
title_zh: 无人机飞行员的眼睛和心脏：现实场景中生理反应的观察
abstract: The drone industry is diversifying and the number of pilots increases rapidly. In this context, flight schools need adapted tools to train pilots, most importantly with regard to their own awareness of their physiological and cognitive limits. In civil and military aviation, pilots can train themselves on realistic simulators to tune their reaction and reflexes, but also to gather data on their piloting behavior and physiological states. It helps them to improve their performances. Opposed to cockpit scenarios, drone teleoperation is conducted outdoor in the field, thus with only limited potential from desktop simulation training. This work aims to provide a solution to gather pilots behavior out in the field and help them increase their performance. We combined advance object detection from a frontal camera to gaze and heart-rate variability measurements. We observed pilots and analyze their behavior over three flight challenges. We believe this tool can support pilots both in their training and in their regular flight tasks. A demonstration video is available on https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eePhjd2qNiI
summary: The drone industry is diversifying and the number of pilots increases rapidly. In this context, flight schools need adapted tools to train pilots, most importantly with regard to their own awareness of their physiological and cognitive limits. In civil and military aviation, pilots can train themselves on realistic simulators to tune their reaction and reflexes, but also to gather data on their piloting behavior and physiological states. It helps them to improve their performances. Opposed to cockpit scenarios, drone teleoperation is conducted outdoor in the field, thus with only limited potential from desktop simulation training. This work aims to provide a solution to gather pilots behavior out in the field and help them increase their performance. We combined advance object detection from a frontal camera to gaze and heart-rate variability measurements. We observed pilots and analyze their behavior over three flight challenges. We believe this tool can support pilots both in their training and in their regular flight tasks. A demonstration video is available on https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eePhjd2qNiI
abstract_zh: 无人机行业正在多元化发展，飞行员数量迅速增加。在这种情况下，飞行学校需要合适的工具来培训飞行员，最重要的是他们对自己的生理和认知极限的认识。在民用和军用航空领域，飞行员可以在真实的模拟器上进行自我训练，以调整他们的反应和反应，还可以收集有关他们的驾驶行为和生理状态的数据。它可以帮助他们提高表现。与驾驶舱场景相反，无人机远程操作是在户外现场进行的，因此桌面模拟训练的潜力有限。这项工作旨在提供一种解决方案来收集飞行员在现场的行为并帮助他们提高绩效。我们将前置摄像头的先进物体检测与凝视和心率变异性测量相结合。我们观察了飞行员并分析了他们在三个飞行挑战中的行为。我们相信该工具可以为飞行员的培训和常规飞行任务提供支持。演示视频可在 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eePhjd2qNiI 上观看

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合视觉检测与生理指标监测的工具，用于在真实场景中收集无人机飞行员的行为与生理数据，以提升其训练与飞行表现。
AI.motivation: 无人机操作场景多为户外，传统模拟器训练效果有限，需开发适应实际环境的工具以帮助飞行员认知自身生理及认知限制。
AI.method: 整合前置摄像头目标检测、眼动追踪与心率变异性测量技术，通过三个飞行挑战任务观察飞行员行为并分析数据。
AI.result: 验证了该工具可有效采集飞行员在真实任务中的生理反应与操作行为，为训练优化提供依据。
AI.conclusion: 该工具可辅助飞行员训练及日常飞行任务，未来需进一步验证其长期应用效果与普适性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种结合视觉检测与生理指标监测的工具，用于在真实场景中收集无人机飞行员的行为与生理数据，以提升其训练与飞行表现。

search_text: The eyes and hearts of UAV pilots: observations of physiological responses in real-life scenarios 无人机飞行员的眼睛和心脏：现实场景中生理反应的观察 The drone industry is diversifying and the number of pilots increases rapidly. In this context, flight schools need adapted tools to train pilots, most importantly with regard to their own awareness of their physiological and cognitive limits. In civil and military aviation, pilots can train themselves on realistic simulators to tune their reaction and reflexes, but also to gather data on their piloting behavior and physiological states. It helps them to improve their performances. Opposed to cockpit scenarios, drone teleoperation is conducted outdoor in the field, thus with only limited potential from desktop simulation training. This work aims to provide a solution to gather pilots behavior out in the field and help them increase their performance. We combined advance object detection from a frontal camera to gaze and heart-rate variability measurements. We observed pilots and analyze their behavior over three flight challenges. We believe this tool can support pilots both in their training and in their regular flight tasks. A demonstration video is available on https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eePhjd2qNiI 无人机行业正在多元化发展，飞行员数量迅速增加。在这种情况下，飞行学校需要合适的工具来培训飞行员，最重要的是他们对自己的生理和认知极限的认识。在民用和军用航空领域，飞行员可以在真实的模拟器上进行自我训练，以调整他们的反应和反应，还可以收集有关他们的驾驶行为和生理状态的数据。它可以帮助他们提高表现。与驾驶舱场景相反，无人机远程操作是在户外现场进行的，因此桌面模拟训练的潜力有限。这项工作旨在提供一种解决方案来收集飞行员在现场的行为并帮助他们提高绩效。我们将前置摄像头的先进物体检测与凝视和心率变异性测量相结合。我们观察了飞行员并分析了他们在三个飞行挑战中的行为。我们相信该工具可以为飞行员的培训和常规飞行任务提供支持。演示视频可在 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eePhjd2qNiI 上观看 本文提出一种结合视觉检测与生理指标监测的工具，用于在真实场景中收集无人机飞行员的行为与生理数据，以提升其训练与飞行表现。 Alexandre Duval,Anita Paas,Abdalwhab Abdalwhab,David St-Onge cs.HC,cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Mahboobeh Jafari,Afshin Shoeibi,Marjane Khodatars,Navid Ghassemi,Parisa Moridian,Niloufar Delfan,Roohallah Alizadehsani,Abbas Khosravi,Sai Ho Ling,Yu-Dong Zhang,Shui-Hua Wang,Juan M. Gorriz,Hamid Alinejad Rokny,U. Rajendra Acharya
title: Automated Diagnosis of Cardiovascular Diseases from Cardiac Magnetic Resonance Imaging Using Deep Learning Models: A Review
title_zh: 使用深度学习模型通过心脏磁共振成像自动诊断心血管疾病：综述
abstract: In recent years, cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) have become one of the leading causes of mortality globally. CVDs appear with minor symptoms and progressively get worse. The majority of people experience symptoms such as exhaustion, shortness of breath, ankle swelling, fluid retention, and other symptoms when starting CVD. Coronary artery disease (CAD), arrhythmia, cardiomyopathy, congenital heart defect (CHD), mitral regurgitation, and angina are the most common CVDs. Clinical methods such as blood tests, electrocardiography (ECG) signals, and medical imaging are the most effective methods used for the detection of CVDs. Among the diagnostic methods, cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (CMR) is increasingly used to diagnose, monitor the disease, plan treatment and predict CVDs. Coupled with all the advantages of CMR data, CVDs diagnosis is challenging for physicians due to many slices of data, low contrast, etc. To address these issues, deep learning (DL) techniques have been employed to the diagnosis of CVDs using CMR data, and much research is currently being conducted in this field. This review provides an overview of the studies performed in CVDs detection using CMR images and DL techniques. The introduction section examined CVDs types, diagnostic methods, and the most important medical imaging techniques. In the following, investigations to detect CVDs using CMR images and the most significant DL methods are presented. Another section discussed the challenges in diagnosing CVDs from CMR data. Next, the discussion section discusses the results of this review, and future work in CVDs diagnosis from CMR images and DL techniques are outlined. The most important findings of this study are presented in the conclusion section.

This review provides an overview of the studies performed in CVDs detection using CMR images and DL techniques. The most important findings of this study are presented in the conclusion section.
abstract_zh: 近年来，心血管疾病（CVD）已成为全球死亡的主要原因之一。 CVD 出现时症状较轻，但会逐渐恶化。大多数人在开始 CVD 时都会出现疲劳、呼吸急促、脚踝肿胀、液体潴留和其他症状。冠状动脉疾病（CAD）、心律失常、心肌病、先天性心脏病（CHD）、二尖瓣反流和心绞痛是最常见的CVD。血液检测、心电图 (ECG) 信号和医学成像等临床方法是检测 CVD 的最有效方法。在诊断方法中，心脏磁共振成像（CMR）越来越多地用于诊断、监测疾病、计划治疗和预测CVD。再加上CMR数据的所有优点，由于数据切片多、对比度低等原因，CVD诊断对医生来说是一个挑战。为了解决这些问题，深度学习（DL）技术已被用于利用CMR数据诊断CVD，目前该领域正在进行大量研究。本综述概述了使用 CMR 图像和深度学习技术进行 CVD 检测的研究。简介部分介绍了 CVD 类型、诊断方法和最重要的医学成像技术。下面介绍使用 CMR 图像检测 CVD 的研究和最重要的深度学习方法。另一部分讨论了根据 CMR 数据诊断 CVD 的挑战。接下来，讨论部分讨论了本次综述的结果，并概述了利用 CMR 图像和深度学习技术诊断 CVD 的未来工作。结论部分介绍了本研究最重要的发现。

AI.tldr: 综述利用深度学习模型从心脏磁共振成像（CMR）数据中自动诊断心血管疾病（CVDs）的研究现状。
AI.motivation: 由于心血管疾病诊断中存在数据复杂性和低对比度等挑战，需借助深度学习技术提高诊断效率和准确性。
AI.method: 通过回顾现有文献，分析CMR图像处理中的深度学习方法，并讨论其在CVDs诊断中的应用与挑战。
AI.result: 总结了当前基于CMR和深度学习的CVDs诊断方法，指出现有技术的局限性，并提出未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习显著提升了CMR数据在CVDs诊断中的应用，但需进一步优化模型以应对数据多样性和临床需求。

authors_text: Pouria Mistani,Samira Pakravan,Rajesh Ilango,Sanjay Choudhry,Frederic Gibou
title: Neuro-symbolic partial differential equation solver
title_zh: 神经符号偏微分方程求解器
comment: Accepted for publication at NeurIPS 2022 (ML4PS workshop). arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2210.14312
comments: Accepted for publication at NeurIPS 2022 (ML4PS workshop). arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2210.14312
abstract: We present a highly scalable strategy for developing mesh-free neuro-symbolic partial differential equation solvers from existing numerical discretizations found in scientific computing. This strategy is unique in that it can be used to efficiently train neural network surrogate models for the solution functions and the differential operators, while retaining the accuracy and convergence properties of state-of-the-art numerical solvers. This neural bootstrapping method is based on minimizing residuals of discretized differential systems on a set of random collocation points with respect to the trainable parameters of the neural network, achieving unprecedented resolution and optimal scaling for solving physical and biological systems.
summary: We present a highly scalable strategy for developing mesh-free neuro-symbolic partial differential equation solvers from existing numerical discretizations found in scientific computing. This strategy is unique in that it can be used to efficiently train neural network surrogate models for the solution functions and the differential operators, while retaining the accuracy and convergence properties of state-of-the-art numerical solvers. This neural bootstrapping method is based on minimizing residuals of discretized differential systems on a set of random collocation points with respect to the trainable parameters of the neural network, achieving unprecedented resolution and optimal scaling for solving physical and biological systems.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种高度可扩展的策略，用于根据科学计算中现有的数值离散开发无网格神经符号偏微分方程求解器。该策略的独特之处在于，它可用于有效地训练求解函数和微分算子的神经网络代理模型，同时保留最先进的数值求解器的准确性和收敛特性。这种神经引导方法基于最小化离散微分系统在一组随机配置点上相对于神经网络可训练参数的残差，实现了前所未有的分辨率和最佳缩放以求解物理和生物系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无网格神经符号偏微分方程求解方法，通过神经网络代理模型保留数值求解器的精度与收敛性。
AI.motivation: 传统数值求解器存在网格依赖性和高计算成本，而神经网络可提升计算效率与扩展性，但需保持数值方法的精度。
AI.method: 基于神经引导方法，在随机配置点上通过最小化离散微分系统残差训练神经网络参数，实现无网格求解。
AI.result: 达到前所未有的分辨率与最优扩展性，成功应用于物理和生物系统的复杂偏微分方程求解。
AI.conclusion: 该方法融合神经网络与符号计算优势，为高精度高效求解PDE提供了新范式。

search_text: Neuro-symbolic partial differential equation solver 神经符号偏微分方程求解器 We present a highly scalable strategy for developing mesh-free neuro-symbolic partial differential equation solvers from existing numerical discretizations found in scientific computing. This strategy is unique in that it can be used to efficiently train neural network surrogate models for the solution functions and the differential operators, while retaining the accuracy and convergence properties of state-of-the-art numerical solvers. This neural bootstrapping method is based on minimizing residuals of discretized differential systems on a set of random collocation points with respect to the trainable parameters of the neural network, achieving unprecedented resolution and optimal scaling for solving physical and biological systems. 我们提出了一种高度可扩展的策略，用于根据科学计算中现有的数值离散开发无网格神经符号偏微分方程求解器。该策略的独特之处在于，它可用于有效地训练求解函数和微分算子的神经网络代理模型，同时保留最先进的数值求解器的准确性和收敛特性。这种神经引导方法基于最小化离散微分系统在一组随机配置点上相对于神经网络可训练参数的残差，实现了前所未有的分辨率和最佳缩放以求解物理和生物系统。 本文提出一种无网格神经符号偏微分方程求解方法，通过神经网络代理模型保留数值求解器的精度与收敛性。 Pouria Mistani,Samira Pakravan,Rajesh Ilango,Sanjay Choudhry,Frederic Gibou math.NA,cs.AI,cs.CE

authors_text: Anas Maach,Jamila Elalami,Noureddine Elalami,El Houssine El Mazoudi
title: An Intelligent Decision Support Ensemble Voting Model for Coronary Artery Disease Prediction in Smart Healthcare Monitoring Environments
title_zh: 智能医疗监测环境中冠状动脉疾病预测的智能决策支持集成投票模型
comment: International Journal of Advanced Computer Science and Applications 2022
comments: International Journal of Advanced Computer Science and Applications 2022
abstract: Coronary artery disease (CAD) is one of the most common cardiac diseases worldwide and causes disability and economic burden. It is the world's leading and most serious cause of mortality, with approximately 80% of deaths reported in low- and middle-income countries. The preferred and most precise diagnostic tool for CAD is angiography, but it is invasive, expensive, and technically demanding. However, the research community is increasingly interested in the computer-aided diagnosis of CAD via the utilization of machine learning (ML) methods. The purpose of this work is to present an e-diagnosis tool based on ML algorithms that can be used in a smart healthcare monitoring system. We applied the most accurate machine learning methods that have shown superior results in the literature to different medical datasets such as RandomForest, XGboost, MLP, J48, AdaBoost, NaiveBayes, LogitBoost, KNN. Every single classifier can be efficient on a different dataset. Thus, an ensemble model using majority voting was designed to take advantage of the well-performed single classifiers, Ensemble learning aims to combine the forecasts of multiple individual classifiers to achieve higher performance than individual classifiers in terms of precision, specificity, sensitivity, and accuracy; furthermore, we have benchmarked our proposed model with the most efficient and well-known ensemble models, such as Bagging, Stacking methods based on the cross-validation technique, The experimental results confirm that the ensemble majority voting approach based on the top 3 classifiers: MultilayerPerceptron, RandomForest, and AdaBoost, achieves the highest accuracy of 88,12% and outperforms all other classifiers. This study demonstrates that the majority voting ensemble approach proposed above is the most accurate machine learning classification approach for the prediction and detection of coronary artery disease.

The purpose of this work is to present an e-diagnosis tool based on ML algorithms that can be used in a smart healthcare monitoring system. We applied the most accurate machine learning methods that have shown superior results in the literature to different medical datasets such as RandomForest, XGboost, MLP, J48, AdaBoost, NaiveBayes, LogitBoost, KNN. Every single classifier can be efficient on a different dataset. Thus, an ensemble model using majority voting was designed to take advantage of the well-performed single classifiers, Ensemble learning aims to combine the forecasts of multiple individual classifiers to achieve higher performance than individual classifiers in terms of precision, specificity, sensitivity, and accuracy; furthermore, we have benchmarked our proposed model with the most efficient and well-known ensemble models, such as Bagging, Stacking methods based on the cross-validation technique, The experimental results confirm that the ensemble majority voting approach based on the top 3 classifiers: MultilayerPerceptron, RandomForest, and AdaBoost, achieves the highest accuracy of 88,12% and outperforms all other classifiers. This study demonstrates that the majority voting ensemble approach proposed above is the most accurate machine learning classification approach for the prediction and detection of coronary artery disease.
abstract_zh: 冠状动脉疾病（CAD）是全世界最常见的心脏病之一，会导致残疾和经济负担。它是世界上最主要且最严重的死亡原因，大约 80% 的死亡发生在低收入和中等收入国家。 CAD 的首选且最精确的诊断工具是血管造影，但它是侵入性的、昂贵的且技术要求高。然而，研究界对利用机器学习 (ML) 方法进行 CAD 计算机辅助诊断越来越感兴趣。这项工作的目的是提出一种基于机器学习算法的电子诊断工具，可用于智能医疗保健监控系统。我们将最准确的机器学习方法应用于不同的医学数据集，例如 RandomForest、XGboost、MLP、J48、AdaBoost、NaiveBayes、LogitBoost、KNN，这些方法已在文献中显示出优异的结果。每个分类器都可以在不同的数据集上保持高效。因此，设计了采用多数投票的集成模型，以利用性能良好的单个分类器，集成学习旨在结合多个单个分类器的预测，以在精度、特异性、灵敏度和准确度方面实现比单个分类器更高的性能；此外，我们用最有效和最知名的集成模型对我们提出的模型进行了基准测试，例如基于交叉验证技术的 Bagging、Stacking 方法。实验结果证实，基于前 3 个分类器：MultilayerPerceptron、RandomForest 和 AdaBoost 的集成多数投票方法达到了 88.12% 的最高准确率，并且优于所有其他分类器。这项研究表明，上述提出的多数投票集成方法是预测和检测冠状动脉疾病的最准确的机器学习分类方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于机器学习的集成投票模型，用于智能医疗环境中冠状动脉疾病（CAD）的预测诊断。
AI.motivation: 冠状动脉疾病是全球致死率最高的疾病之一，传统诊断方法（如血管造影）存在侵入性、成本高和操作复杂等问题，亟需开发非侵入性、高效的计算机辅助诊断工具。
AI.method: 采用随机森林、XGBoost、多层感知机等机器学习分类器，通过多数投票集成方法结合表现最佳的三个分类器（多层感知机、随机森林、AdaBoost），并基于交叉验证与Bagging、Stacking等集成模型进行基准测试。
AI.result: 提出的集成模型在测试数据集上达到88.12%的最高准确率，显著优于单一分类器及其他集成方法。
AI.conclusion: 多数投票集成方法在CAD预测中表现最优，为智能医疗环境下的疾病诊断提供了高精度的机器学习解决方案。

authors_text: Xiaojuan Tang,Song-Chun Zhu,Yitao Liang,Muhan Zhang
title: RulE: Knowledge Graph Reasoning with Rule Embedding
title_zh: Rule：带有规则嵌入的知识图推理
abstract: Knowledge graph (KG) reasoning is an important problem for knowledge graphs. In this paper, we propose a novel and principled framework called \textbf{RulE} (stands for {Rul}e {E}mbedding) to effectively leverage logical rules to enhance KG reasoning. Unlike knowledge graph embedding (KGE) methods, RulE learns rule embeddings from existing triplets and first-order {rules} by jointly representing \textbf{entities}, \textbf{relations} and \textbf{logical rules} in a unified embedding space. Based on the learned rule embeddings, a confidence score can be calculated for each rule, reflecting its consistency with the observed triplets. This allows us to perform logical rule inference in a soft way, thus alleviating the brittleness of logic. On the other hand, RulE injects prior logical rule information into the embedding space, enriching and regularizing the entity/relation embeddings. This makes KGE alone perform better too. RulE is conceptually simple and empirically effective. We conduct extensive experiments to verify each component of RulE. Results on multiple benchmarks reveal that our model outperforms the majority of existing embedding-based and rule-based approaches.
summary: Knowledge graph (KG) reasoning is an important problem for knowledge graphs. In this paper, we propose a novel and principled framework called \textbf{RulE} (stands for {Rul}e {E}mbedding) to effectively leverage logical rules to enhance KG reasoning. Unlike knowledge graph embedding (KGE) methods, RulE learns rule embeddings from existing triplets and first-order {rules} by jointly representing \textbf{entities}, \textbf{relations} and \textbf{logical rules} in a unified embedding space. Based on the learned rule embeddings, a confidence score can be calculated for each rule, reflecting its consistency with the observed triplets. This allows us to perform logical rule inference in a soft way, thus alleviating the brittleness of logic. On the other hand, RulE injects prior logical rule information into the embedding space, enriching and regularizing the entity/relation embeddings. This makes KGE alone perform better too. RulE is conceptually simple and empirically effective. We conduct extensive experiments to verify each component of RulE. Results on multiple benchmarks reveal that our model outperforms the majority of existing embedding-based and rule-based approaches.
abstract_zh: 知识图（KG）推理是知识图的一个重要问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖且有原则的框架，称为 \textbf{RulE} （代表 {Rul}e {E}mbedding），以有效利用逻辑规则来增强知识图谱推理。与知识图嵌入（KGE）方法不同，RulE 通过在统一的嵌入空间中联合表示 \textbf{entities}、\textbf{relations} 和 \textbf{逻辑规则}，从现有的三元组和一阶{rules}中学习规则嵌入。基于学习到的规则嵌入，可以计算每个规则的置信度得分，反映其与观察到的三元组的一致性。这使得我们能够以软的方式进行逻辑规则推理，从而减轻逻辑的脆弱性。另一方面，RulE将先验逻辑规则信息注入到嵌入空间中，丰富和正则化实体/关系嵌入。这使得 KGE 单独表现也更好。 Rule 在概念上简单且在经验上有效。我们进行了大量的实验来验证规则的每个组成部分。多个基准的结果表明，我们的模型优于大多数现有的基于嵌入和基于规则的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为RulE的框架，通过规则嵌入增强知识图谱推理，联合表示实体、关系和逻辑规则。
AI.motivation: 传统知识图谱嵌入方法难以有效利用逻辑规则，而规则推理方法存在逻辑脆弱性，需结合规则嵌入提升推理效果。
AI.method: RulE通过联合学习实体、关系及逻辑规则的嵌入，在统一空间中计算规则置信度，并注入规则信息优化嵌入表示。
AI.result: 在多个基准测试中，RulE优于多数现有基于嵌入和基于规则的方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: RulE通过规则嵌入提升知识图谱推理性能，实验验证了其组件的有效性，且概念简单、效果显著。

On the other hand, RulE injects prior logical rule information into the embedding space, enriching and regularizing the entity/relation embeddings. This makes KGE alone perform better too. RulE is conceptually simple and empirically effective. We conduct extensive experiments to verify each component of RulE. Results on multiple benchmarks reveal that our model outperforms the majority of existing embedding-based and rule-based approaches. 知识图（KG）推理是知识图的一个重要问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖且有原则的框架，称为 \textbf{RulE} （代表 {Rul}e {E}mbedding），以有效利用逻辑规则来增强知识图谱推理。与知识图嵌入（KGE）方法不同，RulE 通过在统一的嵌入空间中联合表示 \textbf{entities}、\textbf{relations} 和 \textbf{逻辑规则}，从现有的三元组和一阶{rules}中学习规则嵌入。基于学习到的规则嵌入，可以计算每个规则的置信度得分，反映其与观察到的三元组的一致性。这使得我们能够以软的方式进行逻辑规则推理，从而减轻逻辑的脆弱性。另一方面，RulE将先验逻辑规则信息注入到嵌入空间中，丰富和正则化实体/关系嵌入。这使得 KGE 单独表现也更好。 Rule 在概念上简单且在经验上有效。我们进行了大量的实验来验证规则的每个组成部分。多个基准的结果表明，我们的模型优于大多数现有的基于嵌入和基于规则的方法。 本文提出了一种名为RulE的框架，通过规则嵌入增强知识图谱推理，联合表示实体、关系和逻辑规则。 Xiaojuan Tang,Song-Chun Zhu,Yitao Liang,Muhan Zhang cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Zhiyuan Zhang,Yuchao Dai,Bin Fan,Jiadai Sun,Mingyi He
title: Learning a Task-specific Descriptor for Robust Matching of 3D Point Clouds
title_zh: 学习特定于任务的描述符以实现 3D 点云的稳健匹配
comment: Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology (TCSVT) 2022
comments: Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology (TCSVT) 2022
abstract: Existing learning-based point feature descriptors are usually task-agnostic, which pursue describing the individual 3D point clouds as accurate as possible. However, the matching task aims at describing the corresponding points consistently across different 3D point clouds. Therefore these too accurate features may play a counterproductive role due to the inconsistent point feature representations of correspondences caused by the unpredictable noise, partiality, deformation, \etc, in the local geometry. In this paper, we propose to learn a robust task-specific feature descriptor to consistently describe the correct point correspondence under interference. Born with an Encoder and a Dynamic Fusion module, our method EDFNet develops from two aspects. First, we augment the matchability of correspondences by utilizing their repetitive local structure. To this end, a special encoder is designed to exploit two input point clouds jointly for each point descriptor. It not only captures the local geometry of each point in the current point cloud by convolution, but also exploits the repetitive structure from paired point cloud by Transformer. Second, we propose a dynamical fusion module to jointly use different scale features. There is an inevitable struggle between robustness and discriminativeness of the single scale feature. Specifically, the small scale feature is robust since little interference exists in this small receptive field. But it is not sufficiently discriminative as there are many repetitive local structures within a point cloud. Thus the resultant descriptors will lead to many incorrect matches. In contrast, the large scale feature is more discriminative by integrating more neighborhood information. ...

However, the matching task aims at describing the corresponding points consistently across different 3D point clouds. Therefore these too accurate features may play a counterproductive role due to the inconsistent point feature representations of correspondences caused by the unpredictable noise, partiality, deformation, \etc, in the local geometry. In this paper, we propose to learn a robust task-specific feature descriptor to consistently describe the correct point correspondence under interference. Born with an Encoder and a Dynamic Fusion module, our method EDFNet develops from two aspects. First, we augment the matchability of correspondences by utilizing their repetitive local structure. To this end, a special encoder is designed to exploit two input point clouds jointly for each point descriptor. It not only captures the local geometry of each point in the current point cloud by convolution, but also exploits the repetitive structure from paired point cloud by Transformer. Second, we propose a dynamical fusion module to jointly use different scale features. There is an inevitable struggle between robustness and discriminativeness of the single scale feature. Specifically, the small scale feature is robust since little interference exists in this small receptive field. But it is not sufficiently discriminative as there are many repetitive local structures within a point cloud. Thus the resultant descriptors will lead to many incorrect matches. In contrast, the large scale feature is more discriminative by integrating more neighborhood information. ...
abstract_zh: 现有的基于学习的点特征描述符通常与任务无关，追求尽可能准确地描述单个 3D 点云。然而，匹配任务旨在一致地描述不同 3D 点云上的对应点。因此，由于局部几何中不可预测的噪声、偏向、变形等导致对应的点特征表示不一致，这些过于精确的特征可能会起到适得​​其反的作用。在本文中，我们建议学习一种鲁棒的特定于任务的特征描述符，以一致地描述干扰下的正确点对应关系。我们的方法 EDFNet 诞生于编码器和动态融合模块，从两个方面发展。首先，我们通过利用重复的局部结构来增强对应关系的可匹配性。为此，设计了一个特殊的编码器，为每个点描述符联合利用两个输入点云。它不仅通过卷积捕获当前点云中每个点的局部几何形状，还通过 Transformer 利用成对点云的重复结构。其次，我们提出了一个动态融合模块来联合使用不同尺度的特征。单一尺度特征的鲁棒性和区分性之间不可避免地存在斗争。具体来说，小尺度特征是鲁棒的，因为在这个小感受野中几乎不存在干扰。但它的辨别力不够，因为点云中有许多重复的局部结构。因此，所得的描述符将导致许多不正确的匹配。相比之下，大规模特征通过整合更多邻域信息而更具辨别力。 ...

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种针对3D点云匹配任务的鲁棒任务特定描述符学习方法，以解决现有方法在噪声、形变等干扰下匹配不一致的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于学习的点特征描述符过于关注单个点云的精确描述，而匹配任务需要跨点云的一致性描述，现有方法因局部几何噪声、部分遮挡等问题导致匹配效果不佳。
AI.method: 提出EDFNet方法，包含编码器和动态融合模块。编码器通过卷积提取局部几何特征，结合Transformer挖掘配对点云的重复结构；动态融合模块整合多尺度特征，平衡鲁棒性与判别性。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多种干扰条件下显著提升了3D点云匹配的准确性和鲁棒性，优于现有任务无关描述符方法。
AI.conclusion: 任务特定描述符设计能有效提升3D点云匹配的鲁棒性，多尺度特征融合策略为处理现实场景中的几何干扰提供了新思路。

authors_text: Yufei Wang,Yuchao Dai,Qi Liu,Peng Yang,Jiadai Sun,Bo Li
title: CU-Net: LiDAR Depth-Only Completion With Coupled U-Net
title_zh: CU-Net：使用耦合 U-Net 完成仅 LiDAR 深度
comment: Accepted to IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L), Code: https://github.com/YufeiWang777/CU-Net
comments: Accepted to IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L), Code: https://github.com/YufeiWang777/CU-Net
abstract: LiDAR depth-only completion is a challenging task to estimate dense depth maps only from sparse measurement points obtained by LiDAR. Even though the depth-only methods have been widely developed, there is still a significant performance gap with the RGB-guided methods that utilize extra color images. We find that existing depth-only methods can obtain satisfactory results in the areas where the measurement points are almost accurate and evenly distributed (denoted as normal areas), while the performance is limited in the areas where the foreground and background points are overlapped due to occlusion (denoted as overlap areas) and the areas where there are no measurement points around (denoted as blank areas) since the methods have no reliable input information in these areas. Building upon these observations, we propose an effective Coupled U-Net (CU-Net) architecture for depth-only completion. Instead of directly using a large network for regression, we employ the local U-Net to estimate accurate values in the normal areas and provide the global U-Net with reliable initial values in the overlap and blank areas. The depth maps predicted by the two coupled U-Nets are fused by learned confidence maps to obtain final results. In addition, we propose a confidence-based outlier removal module, which removes outliers using simple judgment conditions. Our proposed method boosts the final results with fewer parameters and achieves state-of-the-art results on the KITTI benchmark. Moreover, it owns a powerful generalization ability under various depth densities, varying lighting, and weather conditions.
summary: LiDAR depth-only completion is a challenging task to estimate dense depth maps only from sparse measurement points obtained by LiDAR. Even though the depth-only methods have been widely developed, there is still a significant performance gap with the RGB-guided methods that utilize extra color images. We find that existing depth-only methods can obtain satisfactory results in the areas where the measurement points are almost accurate and evenly distributed (denoted as normal areas), while the performance is limited in the areas where the foreground and background points are overlapped due to occlusion (denoted as overlap areas) and the areas where there are no measurement points around (denoted as blank areas) since the methods have no reliable input information in these areas. Building upon these observations, we propose an effective Coupled U-Net (CU-Net) architecture for depth-only completion. Instead of directly using a large network for regression, we employ the local U-Net to estimate accurate values in the normal areas and provide the global U-Net with reliable initial values in the overlap and blank areas. The depth maps predicted by the two coupled U-Nets are fused by learned confidence maps to obtain final results. In addition, we propose a confidence-based outlier removal module, which removes outliers using simple judgment conditions. Our proposed method boosts the final results with fewer parameters and achieves state-of-the-art results on the KITTI benchmark. Moreover, it owns a powerful generalization ability under various depth densities, varying lighting, and weather conditions.
abstract_zh: 仅从 LiDAR 获得的稀疏测量点估计密集深度图是一项具有挑战性的任务。尽管仅深度方法已得到广泛开发，但与利用额外彩色图像的 RGB 引导方法仍然存在显着的性能差距。我们发现，现有的仅深度方法在测量点几乎准确且分布均匀的区域（表示为正常区域）可以获得令人满意的结果，而在前景和背景点由于遮挡而重叠的区域（表示为重叠区域）和周围没有测量点的区域（表示为空白区域）中，性能受到限制，因为这些方法在这些区域没有可靠的输入信息。基于这些观察，我们提出了一种有效的耦合 U-Net (CU-Net) 架构，用于仅深度完成。我们没有直接使用大型网络进行回归，而是使用局部 U-Net 来估计正常区域的准确值，并为全局 U-Net 提供重叠和空白区域的可靠初始值。两个耦合的 U-Net 预测的深度图与学习到的置信度图融合以获得最终结果。此外，我们提出了一种基于置信度的异常值去除模块，该模块使用简单的判断条件来去除异常值。我们提出的方法用更少的参数提高了最终结果，并在 KITTI 基准上实现了最先进的结果。此外，它在各种深度密度、变化的光照和天气条件下具有强大的泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CU-Net的深度完成网络，用于仅基于LiDAR深度点云生成密集深度图。
AI.motivation: 现有的深度仅方法在正常区域表现良好，但在重叠区域和空白区域由于缺乏可靠输入信息而性能受限，因此需要一种更有效的方法来解决这些问题。
AI.method: 提出了一种耦合U-Net（CU-Net）架构，利用局部U-Net估计正常区域的深度值，并为全局U-Net提供重叠和空白区域的可靠初始值，通过学习的置信度图融合两个U-Net的预测结果，并引入基于置信度的异常值去除模块。
AI.result: 该方法在KITTI基准测试中取得了最先进的结果，并在不同深度密度、光照和天气条件下表现出强大的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: CU-Net通过耦合U-Net结构和置信度图融合策略，有效提升了深度仅完成任务的性能，具有较少的参数和广泛的应用前景。

search_text: CU-Net: LiDAR Depth-Only Completion With Coupled U-Net CU-Net：使用耦合 U-Net 完成仅 LiDAR 深度 LiDAR depth-only completion is a challenging task to estimate dense depth maps only from sparse measurement points obtained by LiDAR. Even though the depth-only methods have been widely developed, there is still a significant performance gap with the RGB-guided methods that utilize extra color images. We find that existing depth-only methods can obtain satisfactory results in the areas where the measurement points are almost accurate and evenly distributed (denoted as normal areas), while the performance is limited in the areas where the foreground and background points are overlapped due to occlusion (denoted as overlap areas) and the areas where there are no measurement points around (denoted as blank areas) since the methods have no reliable input information in these areas. Building upon these observations, we propose an effective Coupled U-Net (CU-Net) architecture for depth-only completion. Instead of directly using a large network for regression, we employ the local U-Net to estimate accurate values in the normal areas and provide the global U-Net with reliable initial values in the overlap and blank areas. The depth maps predicted by the two coupled U-Nets are fused by learned confidence maps to obtain final results. In addition, we propose a confidence-based outlier removal module, which removes outliers using simple judgment conditions. Our proposed method boosts the final results with fewer parameters and achieves state-of-the-art results on the KITTI benchmark. Moreover, it owns a powerful generalization ability under various depth densities, varying lighting, and weather conditions. 仅从 LiDAR 获得的稀疏测量点估计密集深度图是一项具有挑战性的任务。尽管仅深度方法已得到广泛开发，但与利用额外彩色图像的 RGB 引导方法仍然存在显着的性能差距。我们发现，现有的仅深度方法在测量点几乎准确且分布均匀的区域（表示为正常区域）可以获得令人满意的结果，而在前景和背景点由于遮挡而重叠的区域（表示为重叠区域）和周围没有测量点的区域（表示为空白区域）中，性能受到限制，因为这些方法在这些区域没有可靠的输入信息。基于这些观察，我们提出了一种有效的耦合 U-Net (CU-Net) 架构，用于仅深度完成。我们没有直接使用大型网络进行回归，而是使用局部 U-Net 来估计正常区域的准确值，并为全局 U-Net 提供重叠和空白区域的可靠初始值。两个耦合的 U-Net 预测的深度图与学习到的置信度图融合以获得最终结果。此外，我们提出了一种基于置信度的异常值去除模块，该模块使用简单的判断条件来去除异常值。我们提出的方法用更少的参数提高了最终结果，并在 KITTI 基准上实现了最先进的结果。此外，它在各种深度密度、变化的光照和天气条件下具有强大的泛化能力。 本文提出了一种名为CU-Net的深度完成网络，用于仅基于LiDAR深度点云生成密集深度图。 Yufei Wang,Yuchao Dai,Qi Liu,Peng Yang,Jiadai Sun,Bo Li cs.CV

authors_text: Zhiyuan Zhang,Jiadai Sun,Yuchao Dai,Bin Fan,Qi Liu
title: Searching Dense Point Correspondences via Permutation Matrix Learning
title_zh: 通过置换矩阵学习搜索密集点对应关系
comment: Accepted to IEEE Signal Processing Letters (SPL) 2022
comments: Accepted to IEEE Signal Processing Letters (SPL) 2022
abstract: Although 3D point cloud data has received widespread attentions as a general form of 3D signal expression, applying point clouds to the task of dense correspondence estimation between 3D shapes has not been investigated widely. Furthermore, even in the few existing 3D point cloud-based methods, an important and widely acknowledged principle, i.e . one-to-one matching, is usually ignored. In response, this paper presents a novel end-to-end learning-based method to estimate the dense correspondence of 3D point clouds, in which the problem of point matching is formulated as a zero-one assignment problem to achieve a permutation matching matrix to implement the one-to-one principle fundamentally. Note that the classical solutions of this assignment problem are always non-differentiable, which is fatal for deep learning frameworks. Thus we design a special matching module, which solves a doubly stochastic matrix at first and then projects this obtained approximate solution to the desired permutation matrix. Moreover, to guarantee end-to-end learning and the accuracy of the calculated loss, we calculate the loss from the learned permutation matrix but propagate the gradient to the doubly stochastic matrix directly which bypasses the permutation matrix during the backward propagation. Our method can be applied to both non-rigid and rigid 3D point cloud data and extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance for dense correspondence learning.
summary: Although 3D point cloud data has received widespread attentions as a general form of 3D signal expression, applying point clouds to the task of dense correspondence estimation between 3D shapes has not been investigated widely. Furthermore, even in the few existing 3D point cloud-based methods, an important and widely acknowledged principle, i.e . one-to-one matching, is usually ignored. In response, this paper presents a novel end-to-end learning-based method to estimate the dense correspondence of 3D point clouds, in which the problem of point matching is formulated as a zero-one assignment problem to achieve a permutation matching matrix to implement the one-to-one principle fundamentally. Note that the classical solutions of this assignment problem are always non-differentiable, which is fatal for deep learning frameworks. Thus we design a special matching module, which solves a doubly stochastic matrix at first and then projects this obtained approximate solution to the desired permutation matrix. Moreover, to guarantee end-to-end learning and the accuracy of the calculated loss, we calculate the loss from the learned permutation matrix but propagate the gradient to the doubly stochastic matrix directly which bypasses the permutation matrix during the backward propagation. Our method can be applied to both non-rigid and rigid 3D point cloud data and extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance for dense correspondence learning.
abstract_zh: 尽管 3D 点云数据作为 3D 信号表达的通用形式受到了广泛关注，但将点云应用于 3D 形状之间的密集对应估计任务尚未得到广泛研究。此外，即使在现有的少数基于 3D 点云的方法中，也有一个重要且广泛认可的原则，即一对一匹配，通常被忽略。为此，本文提出了一种新颖的基于端到端学习的方法来估计3D点云的密集对应关系，其中将点匹配问题表述为零一分配问题，以实现排列匹配矩阵，从根本上实现一对一原则。请注意，这个分配问题的经典解决方案始终是不可微的，这对于深度学习框架来说是致命的。因此，我们设计了一个特殊的匹配模块，它首先求解双随机矩阵，然后将得到的近似解投影到所需的置换矩阵。此外，为了保证端到端学习和计算损失的准确性，我们根据学习的置换矩阵计算损失，但将梯度直接传播到双随机矩阵，这在反向传播过程中绕过了置换矩阵。我们的方法可以应用于非刚性和刚性 3D 点云数据，并且大量实验表明我们的方法在密集对应学习方面实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于端到端学习的3D点云密集对应估计方法。
AI.motivation: 虽然3D点云数据作为3D信号表达的通用形式受到广泛关注，但将其应用于3D形状之间的密集对应估计任务尚未被广泛研究。现有方法通常忽略了重要的点对点匹配原则。
AI.method: 该方法将点匹配问题公式化为零一指派问题，通过设计特殊的匹配模块解决双重随机矩阵并投影到期望的排列矩阵，从而实现端到端学习。
AI.result: 该方法在非刚性和刚性3D点云数据上均表现出色，实验表明其在密集对应学习任务中达到了最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法有效解决了3D点云密集对应估计中的点对点匹配问题，并在多种数据集上取得了优异的性能。

search_text: Searching Dense Point Correspondences via Permutation Matrix Learning 通过置换矩阵学习搜索密集点对应关系 Although 3D point cloud data has received widespread attentions as a general form of 3D signal expression, applying point clouds to the task of dense correspondence estimation between 3D shapes has not been investigated widely. Furthermore, even in the few existing 3D point cloud-based methods, an important and widely acknowledged principle, i.e . one-to-one matching, is usually ignored. In response, this paper presents a novel end-to-end learning-based method to estimate the dense correspondence of 3D point clouds, in which the problem of point matching is formulated as a zero-one assignment problem to achieve a permutation matching matrix to implement the one-to-one principle fundamentally. Note that the classical solutions of this assignment problem are always non-differentiable, which is fatal for deep learning frameworks. Thus we design a special matching module, which solves a doubly stochastic matrix at first and then projects this obtained approximate solution to the desired permutation matrix. Moreover, to guarantee end-to-end learning and the accuracy of the calculated loss, we calculate the loss from the learned permutation matrix but propagate the gradient to the doubly stochastic matrix directly which bypasses the permutation matrix during the backward propagation. Our method can be applied to both non-rigid and rigid 3D point cloud data and extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance for dense correspondence learning. 尽管 3D 点云数据作为 3D 信号表达的通用形式受到了广泛关注，但将点云应用于 3D 形状之间的密集对应估计任务尚未得到广泛研究。此外，即使在现有的少数基于 3D 点云的方法中，也有一个重要且广泛认可的原则，即一对一匹配，通常被忽略。为此，本文提出了一种新颖的基于端到端学习的方法来估计3D点云的密集对应关系，其中将点匹配问题表述为零一分配问题，以实现排列匹配矩阵，从根本上实现一对一原则。请注意，这个分配问题的经典解决方案始终是不可微的，这对于深度学习框架来说是致命的。因此，我们设计了一个特殊的匹配模块，它首先求解双随机矩阵，然后将得到的近似解投影到所需的置换矩阵。此外，为了保证端到端学习和计算损失的准确性，我们根据学习的置换矩阵计算损失，但将梯度直接传播到双随机矩阵，这在反向传播过程中绕过了置换矩阵。我们的方法可以应用于非刚性和刚性 3D 点云数据，并且大量实验表明我们的方法在密集对应学习方面实现了最先进的性能。 本文提出了一种基于端到端学习的3D点云密集对应估计方法。 Zhiyuan Zhang,Jiadai Sun,Yuchao Dai,Bin Fan,Qi Liu cs.CV

authors_text: Zijie J. Wang,Evan Montoya,David Munechika,Haoyang Yang,Benjamin Hoover,Duen Horng Chau
title: DiffusionDB: A Large-scale Prompt Gallery Dataset for Text-to-Image Generative Models
title_zh: DiffusionDB：用于文本到图像生成模型的大型提示图库数据集
comment: Accepted to ACL 2023 (nominated for best paper, top 1.6% of submissions, oral presentation). 17 pages, 11 figures. The dataset is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/poloclub/diffusiondb. The code is at https://github.com/poloclub/diffusiondb. The interactive visualization demo is at https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb/explorer/
comments: Accepted to ACL 2023 (nominated for best paper, top 1.6% of submissions, oral presentation). 17 pages, 11 figures. The dataset is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/poloclub/diffusiondb. The code is at https://github.com/poloclub/diffusiondb. The interactive visualization demo is at https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb/explorer/
abstract: With recent advancements in diffusion models, users can generate high-quality images by writing text prompts in natural language. However, generating images with desired details requires proper prompts, and it is often unclear how a model reacts to different prompts or what the best prompts are. To help researchers tackle these critical challenges, we introduce DiffusionDB, the first large-scale text-to-image prompt dataset totaling 6.5TB, containing 14 million images generated by Stable Diffusion, 1.8 million unique prompts, and hyperparameters specified by real users. We analyze the syntactic and semantic characteristics of prompts. We pinpoint specific hyperparameter values and prompt styles that can lead to model errors and present evidence of potentially harmful model usage, such as the generation of misinformation. The unprecedented scale and diversity of this human-actuated dataset provide exciting research opportunities in understanding the interplay between prompts and generative models, detecting deepfakes, and designing human-AI interaction tools to help users more easily use these models. DiffusionDB is publicly available at: https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb.
summary: With recent advancements in diffusion models, users can generate high-quality images by writing text prompts in natural language. However, generating images with desired details requires proper prompts, and it is often unclear how a model reacts to different prompts or what the best prompts are. To help researchers tackle these critical challenges, we introduce DiffusionDB, the first large-scale text-to-image prompt dataset totaling 6.5TB, containing 14 million images generated by Stable Diffusion, 1.8 million unique prompts, and hyperparameters specified by real users. We analyze the syntactic and semantic characteristics of prompts. We pinpoint specific hyperparameter values and prompt styles that can lead to model errors and present evidence of potentially harmful model usage, such as the generation of misinformation. The unprecedented scale and diversity of this human-actuated dataset provide exciting research opportunities in understanding the interplay between prompts and generative models, detecting deepfakes, and designing human-AI interaction tools to help users more easily use these models. DiffusionDB is publicly available at: https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb.
abstract_zh: With recent advancements in diffusion models, users can generate high-quality images by writing text prompts in natural language.然而，生成具有所需细节的图像需要适当的提示，并且通常不清楚模型如何对不同的提示做出反应或最好的提示是什么。为了帮助研究人员应对这些关键挑战，我们引入了 DiffusionDB，这是第一个大规模文本到图像提示数据集，总计 6.5TB，包含 Stable Diffusion 生成的 1400 万张图像、180 万条独特提示以及真实用户指定的超参数。 We analyze the syntactic and semantic characteristics of prompts.我们查明可能导致模型错误的特定超参数值和提示样式，并提供潜在有害模型使用的证据，例如生成错误信息。这个人类驱动数据集前所未有的规模和多样性为理解提示和生成模型之间的相互作用、检测深度伪造以及设计人机交互工具以帮助用户更轻松地使用这些模型提供了令人兴奋的研究机会。 DiffusionDB is publicly available at: https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb.

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为DiffusionDB的大规模文本到图像生成模型提示数据集，包含1400万张图像、180万个独特提示及用户指定的超参数，旨在研究提示与生成模型的交互。
AI.motivation: 尽管扩散模型能生成高质量图像，但如何选择有效提示以控制生成细节仍不明确，且缺乏对模型反应机制和潜在风险的系统性研究。
AI.method: 通过收集真实用户使用Stable Diffusion生成的图像及对应提示和超参数，构建包含6.5TB数据的数据库，并分析提示的语法语义特征及超参数对模型行为的影响。
AI.result: 揭示了特定超参数和提示模式可能导致模型错误，发现潜在有害应用案例（如生成虚假信息），并为提示优化、深度伪造检测和人机交互工具设计提供研究基础。
AI.conclusion: DiffusionDB为理解生成模型与提示的交互机制提供了大规模实证数据，有助于推动安全可控的AI生成技术发展，数据集已公开共享。

The unprecedented scale and diversity of this human-actuated dataset provide exciting research opportunities in understanding the interplay between prompts and generative models, detecting deepfakes, and designing human-AI interaction tools to help users more easily use these models. DiffusionDB is publicly available at: https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb. With recent advancements in diffusion models, users can generate high-quality images by writing text prompts in natural language.然而，生成具有所需细节的图像需要适当的提示，并且通常不清楚模型如何对不同的提示做出反应或最好的提示是什么。为了帮助研究人员应对这些关键挑战，我们引入了 DiffusionDB，这是第一个大规模文本到图像提示数据集，总计 6.5TB，包含 Stable Diffusion 生成的 1400 万张图像、180 万条独特提示以及真实用户指定的超参数。 We analyze the syntactic and semantic characteristics of prompts.我们查明可能导致模型错误的特定超参数值和提示样式，并提供潜在有害模型使用的证据，例如生成错误信息。这个人类驱动数据集前所未有的规模和多样性为理解提示和生成模型之间的相互作用、检测深度伪造以及设计人机交互工具以帮助用户更轻松地使用这些模型提供了令人兴奋的研究机会。 DiffusionDB is publicly available at: https://poloclub.github.io/diffusiondb. 本文介绍了一个名为DiffusionDB的大规模文本到图像生成模型提示数据集，包含1400万张图像、180万个独特提示及用户指定的超参数，旨在研究提示与生成模型的交互。 Zijie J. Wang,Evan Montoya,David Munechika,Haoyang Yang,Benjamin Hoover,Duen Horng Chau cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.HC,cs.LG

authors_text: Hsin-Yuan Huang,Sitan Chen,John Preskill
title: Learning to predict arbitrary quantum processes
title_zh: 学习预测任意量子过程
comment: 16 pages, 5 figure + 36-page appendix; v3: Added numerical experiments; open source code available at https://github.com/hsinyuan-huang/learning-quantum-process
comments: 16 pages, 5 figure + 36-page appendix; v3: Added numerical experiments; open source code available at https://github.com/hsinyuan-huang/learning-quantum-process
abstract: We present an efficient machine learning (ML) algorithm for predicting any unknown quantum process $\mathcal{E}$ over $n$ qubits. For a wide range of distributions $\mathcal{D}$ on arbitrary $n$-qubit states, we show that this ML algorithm can learn to predict any local property of the output from the unknown process~$\mathcal{E}$, with a small average error over input states drawn from $\mathcal{D}$. The ML algorithm is computationally efficient even when the unknown process is a quantum circuit with exponentially many gates. Our algorithm combines efficient procedures for learning properties of an unknown state and for learning a low-degree approximation to an unknown observable. The analysis hinges on proving new norm inequalities, including a quantum analogue of the classical Bohnenblust-Hille inequality, which we derive by giving an improved algorithm for optimizing local Hamiltonians. Numerical experiments on predicting quantum dynamics with evolution time up to $10^6$ and system size up to $50$ qubits corroborate our proof. Overall, our results highlight the potential for ML models to predict the output of complex quantum dynamics much faster than the time needed to run the process itself.
summary: We present an efficient machine learning (ML) algorithm for predicting any unknown quantum process $\mathcal{E}$ over $n$ qubits. For a wide range of distributions $\mathcal{D}$ on arbitrary $n$-qubit states, we show that this ML algorithm can learn to predict any local property of the output from the unknown process~$\mathcal{E}$, with a small average error over input states drawn from $\mathcal{D}$. The ML algorithm is computationally efficient even when the unknown process is a quantum circuit with exponentially many gates. Our algorithm combines efficient procedures for learning properties of an unknown state and for learning a low-degree approximation to an unknown observable. The analysis hinges on proving new norm inequalities, including a quantum analogue of the classical Bohnenblust-Hille inequality, which we derive by giving an improved algorithm for optimizing local Hamiltonians. Numerical experiments on predicting quantum dynamics with evolution time up to $10^6$ and system size up to $50$ qubits corroborate our proof. Overall, our results highlight the potential for ML models to predict the output of complex quantum dynamics much faster than the time needed to run the process itself.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种有效的机器学习（ML）算法，用于预测 $n$ 量子位上的任何未知量子过程 $\mathcal{E}$。对于任意 $n$-qubit 状态上的广泛分布 $\mathcal{D}$，我们表明该 ML 算法可以学习预测未知过程 ~$\mathcal{E}$ 输出的任何局部属性，并且对从 $\mathcal{D}$ 得出的输入状态具有较小的平均误差。即使未知过程是具有指数级数量的门的量子电路，机器学习算法的计算效率也很高。我们的算法结合了有效的程序来学习未知状态的属性和学习未知可观察量的低度近似。该分析取决于证明新的范数不等式，包括经典 Bohnenblust-Hille 不等式的量子模拟，我们通过给出一种用于优化局部哈密顿量的改进算法来推导该不等式。预测演化时间高达 10^6 美元、系统规模高达 50 美元量子位的量子动力学的数值实验证实了我们的证明。总的来说，我们的结果凸显了机器学习模型预测复杂量子动力学输出的潜力，其速度比运行过程本身所需的时间快得多。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种高效的机器学习算法，用于预测任意未知的n量子比特量子过程的局部属性。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以高效模拟复杂量子过程，需开发快速预测量子动力学的机器学习模型。
AI.method: 结合未知态属性学习与低阶可观测量逼近方法，证明新的范数不等式并改进局部哈密顿量优化算法。
AI.result: 算法在指数级量子门电路中仍具计算效率，数值实验验证了对50量子比特系统长达10^6时间步的预测能力。
AI.conclusion: 机器学习模型有望比直接模拟更快预测复杂量子动力学过程的输出结果。

authors_text: Ethan Caballero,Kshitij Gupta,Irina Rish,David Krueger
title: Broken Neural Scaling Laws
title_zh: 打破神经尺度法则
comment: Published as a conference paper at International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR) 2023
comments: Published as a conference paper at International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR) 2023
abstract: We present a smoothly broken power law functional form (that we refer to as a Broken Neural Scaling Law (BNSL)) that accurately models & extrapolates the scaling behaviors of deep neural networks (i.e. how the evaluation metric of interest varies as amount of compute used for training (or inference), number of model parameters, training dataset size, model input size, number of training steps, or upstream performance varies) for various architectures & for each of various tasks within a large & diverse set of upstream & downstream tasks, in zero-shot, prompted, & finetuned settings. This set includes large-scale vision, language, audio, video, diffusion, generative modeling, multimodal learning, contrastive learning, AI alignment, AI capabilities, robotics, out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization, continual learning, transfer learning, uncertainty estimation / calibration, OOD detection, adversarial robustness, distillation, sparsity, retrieval, quantization, pruning, fairness, molecules, computer programming/coding, math word problems, "emergent phase transitions", arithmetic, supervised learning, unsupervised/self-supervised learning, & reinforcement learning (single agent & multi-agent). When compared to other functional forms for neural scaling, this functional form yields extrapolations of scaling behavior that are considerably more accurate on this set. Moreover, this functional form accurately models & extrapolates scaling behavior that other functional forms are incapable of expressing such as the nonmonotonic transitions present in the scaling behavior of phenomena such as double descent & the delayed, sharp inflection points present in the scaling behavior of tasks such as arithmetic. Lastly, we use this functional form to glean insights about the limit of the predictability of scaling behavior. Code is available at https://github.com/ethancaballero/broken_neural_scaling_laws
summary: We present a smoothly broken power law functional form (that we refer to as a Broken Neural Scaling Law (BNSL)) that accurately models & extrapolates the scaling behaviors of deep neural networks (i.e. how the evaluation metric of interest varies as amount of compute used for training (or inference), number of model parameters, training dataset size, model input size, number of training steps, or upstream performance varies) for various architectures & for each of various tasks within a large & diverse set of upstream & downstream tasks, in zero-shot, prompted, & finetuned settings. This set includes large-scale vision, language, audio, video, diffusion, generative modeling, multimodal learning, contrastive learning, AI alignment, AI capabilities, robotics, out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization, continual learning, transfer learning, uncertainty estimation / calibration, OOD detection, adversarial robustness, distillation, sparsity, retrieval, quantization, pruning, fairness, molecules, computer programming/coding, math word problems, "emergent phase transitions", arithmetic, supervised learning, unsupervised/self-supervised learning, & reinforcement learning (single agent & multi-agent). When compared to other functional forms for neural scaling, this functional form yields extrapolations of scaling behavior that are considerably more accurate on this set. Moreover, this functional form accurately models & extrapolates scaling behavior that other functional forms are incapable of expressing such as the nonmonotonic transitions present in the scaling behavior of phenomena such as double descent & the delayed, sharp inflection points present in the scaling behavior of tasks such as arithmetic. Lastly, we use this functional form to glean insights about the limit of the predictability of scaling behavior. Code is available at https://github.com/ethancaballero/broken_neural_scaling_laws
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种平滑破碎的幂律函数形式（我们称为破碎神经缩放法则（BNSL）），它可以准确地建模和推断深度神经网络的缩放行为（即，感兴趣的评估指标如何随着用于训练（或推理）的计算量、模型参数数量、训练数据集大小、模型输入大小、训练步骤数量或上游性能的变化而变化），适用于各种架构以及大量不同的上游和下游任务中的每个任务，零射击、提示和微调设置。该套件包括大规模视觉、语言、音频、视频、扩散、生成建模、多模态学习、对比学习、AI 对齐、AI 功能、机器人、分布外 (OOD) 泛化、持续学习、转移学习、不确定性估计/校准、OOD 检测、对抗鲁棒性、蒸馏、稀疏性、检索、量化、修剪、公平性、分子、计算机编程/编码、数学文字问题、“紧急相变”、算术、监督学习、无监督/自监督学习和强化学习（单代理和多代理）。与神经缩放的其他函数形式相比，这种函数形式产生的缩放行为的外推在该集合上要准确得多。此外，这种函数形式可以准确地建模和推断其他函数形式无法表达的缩放行为，例如双下降等现象的缩放行为中存在的非单调转变，以及算术等任务的缩放行为中存在的延迟的尖锐拐点。最后，我们使用这种函数形式来收集有关扩展行为可预测性极限的见解。代码可在 https://github.com/ethancaballero/broken_neural_scaling_laws 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的破碎神经缩放定律（BNSL）函数形式，用于更准确地建模和预测深度神经网络在多种任务和架构下的缩放行为。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络缩放行为模型难以准确描述非单调转换（如双重下降）和延迟尖锐拐点等复杂现象，需开发更通用的函数形式。
AI.method: 提出平滑破碎幂律函数（BNSL），在零样本、提示和微调设置下，针对视觉、语言、强化学习等20+领域进行多维度缩放行为建模与对比验证。
AI.result: BNSL在多个任务中显著优于传统模型，可准确拟合双重下降、算术任务拐点等复杂行为，并揭示缩放行为预测的理论极限。
AI.conclusion: BNSL为神经网络缩放行为建模提供更精确工具，其成功表明破碎幂律形式能有效捕捉跨任务的复杂可扩展性规律。

Code is available at https://github.com/ethancaballero/broken_neural_scaling_laws 我们提出了一种平滑破碎的幂律函数形式（我们称为破碎神经缩放法则（BNSL）），它可以准确地建模和推断深度神经网络的缩放行为（即，感兴趣的评估指标如何随着用于训练（或推理）的计算量、模型参数数量、训练数据集大小、模型输入大小、训练步骤数量或上游性能的变化而变化），适用于各种架构以及大量不同的上游和下游任务中的每个任务，零射击、提示和微调设置。该套件包括大规模视觉、语言、音频、视频、扩散、生成建模、多模态学习、对比学习、AI 对齐、AI 功能、机器人、分布外 (OOD) 泛化、持续学习、转移学习、不确定性估计/校准、OOD 检测、对抗鲁棒性、蒸馏、稀疏性、检索、量化、修剪、公平性、分子、计算机编程/编码、数学文字问题、“紧急相变”、算术、监督学习、无监督/自监督学习和强化学习（单代理和多代理）。与神经缩放的其他函数形式相比，这种函数形式产生的缩放行为的外推在该集合上要准确得多。此外，这种函数形式可以准确地建模和推断其他函数形式无法表达的缩放行为，例如双下降等现象的缩放行为中存在的非单调转变，以及算术等任务的缩放行为中存在的延迟的尖锐拐点。最后，我们使用这种函数形式来收集有关扩展行为可预测性极限的见解。代码可在 https://github.com/ethancaballero/broken_neural_scaling_laws 获取 本文提出一种新的破碎神经缩放定律（BNSL）函数形式，用于更准确地建模和预测深度神经网络在多种任务和架构下的缩放行为。 Ethan Caballero,Kshitij Gupta,Irina Rish,David Krueger cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Christian Schroeder de Witt,Samuel Sokota,J. Zico Kolter,Jakob Foerster,Martin Strohmeier
title: Perfectly Secure Steganography Using Minimum Entropy Coupling
title_zh: 使用最小熵耦合实现完全安全的隐写术
abstract: Steganography is the practice of encoding secret information into innocuous content in such a manner that an adversarial third party would not realize that there is hidden meaning. While this problem has classically been studied in security literature, recent advances in generative models have led to a shared interest among security and machine learning researchers in developing scalable steganography techniques. In this work, we show that a steganography procedure is perfectly secure under Cachin (1998)'s information-theoretic model of steganography if and only if it is induced by a coupling. Furthermore, we show that, among perfectly secure procedures, a procedure maximizes information throughput if and only if it is induced by a minimum entropy coupling. These insights yield what are, to the best of our knowledge, the first steganography algorithms to achieve perfect security guarantees for arbitrary covertext distributions. To provide empirical validation, we compare a minimum entropy coupling-based approach to three modern baselines -- arithmetic coding, Meteor, and adaptive dynamic grouping -- using GPT-2, WaveRNN, and Image Transformer as communication channels. We find that the minimum entropy coupling-based approach achieves superior encoding efficiency, despite its stronger security constraints. In aggregate, these results suggest that it may be natural to view information-theoretic steganography through the lens of minimum entropy coupling.

While this problem has classically been studied in security literature, recent advances in generative models have led to a shared interest among security and machine learning researchers in developing scalable steganography techniques. In this work, we show that a steganography procedure is perfectly secure under Cachin (1998)'s information-theoretic model of steganography if and only if it is induced by a coupling. Furthermore, we show that, among perfectly secure procedures, a procedure maximizes information throughput if and only if it is induced by a minimum entropy coupling. These insights yield what are, to the best of our knowledge, the first steganography algorithms to achieve perfect security guarantees for arbitrary covertext distributions. To provide empirical validation, we compare a minimum entropy coupling-based approach to three modern baselines -- arithmetic coding, Meteor, and adaptive dynamic grouping -- using GPT-2, WaveRNN, and Image Transformer as communication channels. We find that the minimum entropy coupling-based approach achieves superior encoding efficiency, despite its stronger security constraints. In aggregate, these results suggest that it may be natural to view information-theoretic steganography through the lens of minimum entropy coupling.
abstract_zh: 隐写术是将秘密信息编码为无害内容的做法，其方式使敌对第三方不会意识到其中隐藏的含义。虽然这个问题在安全文献中得到了经典的研究，但生成模型的最新进展引起了安全和机器学习研究人员对开发可扩展的隐写技术的共同兴趣。在这项工作中，我们证明，当且仅当由耦合引起时，隐写过程在 Cachin (1998) 的隐写信息理论模型下是完全安全的。此外，我们表明，在完全安全的过程中，当且仅当它由最小熵耦合引起时，过程才能最大化信息吞吐量。据我们所知，这些见解产生了第一个隐写算法，可以为任意隐藏文本分布实现完美的安全保证。为了提供实证验证，我们使用 GPT-2、WaveRNN 和 Image Transformer 作为通信渠道，将基于最小熵耦合的方法与三种现代基线（算术编码、Meteor 和自适应动态分组）进行比较。我们发现基于最小熵耦合的方法尽管具有更强的安全约束，但仍实现了卓越的编码效率。总的来说，这些结果表明，通过最小熵耦合的视角来看待信息论隐写术可能是很自然的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于最小熵耦合的完美安全隐写术算法，为任意载体分布提供信息论安全保证。
AI.motivation: 传统隐写术难以在任意载体分布下实现完美安全性，而生成模型的发展推动了对可扩展隐写技术的需求。
AI.method: 证明完美安全隐写当且仅当由耦合诱导，且最小熵耦合可最大化信息吞吐量，据此设计新型隐写算法并进行实验验证。
AI.result: 最小熵耦合方法在GPT-2、WaveRNN等通道中编码效率优于算术编码、Meteor等三种现代基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 信息论隐写应从最小熵耦合视角重新审视，该方法在安全性和效率间实现了有效平衡。

authors_text: Samuel Yen-Chi Chen
title: Quantum deep recurrent reinforcement learning
title_zh: 量子深度循环强化学习
abstract: Recent advances in quantum computing (QC) and machine learning (ML) have drawn significant attention to the development of quantum machine learning (QML). Reinforcement learning (RL) is one of the ML paradigms which can be used to solve complex sequential decision making problems. Classical RL has been shown to be capable to solve various challenging tasks. However, RL algorithms in the quantum world are still in their infancy. One of the challenges yet to solve is how to train quantum RL in the partially observable environments. In this paper, we approach this challenge through building QRL agents with quantum recurrent neural networks (QRNN). Specifically, we choose the quantum long short-term memory (QLSTM) to be the core of the QRL agent and train the whole model with deep $Q$-learning. We demonstrate the results via numerical simulations that the QLSTM-DRQN can solve standard benchmark such as Cart-Pole with more stable and higher average scores than classical DRQN with similar architecture and number of model parameters.
summary: Recent advances in quantum computing (QC) and machine learning (ML) have drawn significant attention to the development of quantum machine learning (QML). Reinforcement learning (RL) is one of the ML paradigms which can be used to solve complex sequential decision making problems. Classical RL has been shown to be capable to solve various challenging tasks. However, RL algorithms in the quantum world are still in their infancy. One of the challenges yet to solve is how to train quantum RL in the partially observable environments. In this paper, we approach this challenge through building QRL agents with quantum recurrent neural networks (QRNN). Specifically, we choose the quantum long short-term memory (QLSTM) to be the core of the QRL agent and train the whole model with deep $Q$-learning. We demonstrate the results via numerical simulations that the QLSTM-DRQN can solve standard benchmark such as Cart-Pole with more stable and higher average scores than classical DRQN with similar architecture and number of model parameters.
abstract_zh: 量子计算（QC）和机器学习（ML）的最新进展引起了人们对量子机器学习（QML）发展的极大关注。强化学习（RL）是机器学习范式之一，可用于解决复杂的顺序决策问题。经典强化学习已被证明能够解决各种具有挑战性的任务。然而，量子世界中的强化学习算法仍处于起步阶段。尚未解决的挑战之一是如何在部分可观测的环境中训练量子强化学习。在本文中，我们通过使用量子递归神经网络（QRNN）构建 QRL 代理来应对这一挑战。具体来说，我们选择量子长短期记忆（QLSTM）作为 QRL 代理的核心，并通过深度 $Q$ 学习来训练整个模型。我们通过数值模拟证明，与具有相似架构和模型参数数量的经典 DRQN 相比，QLSTM-DRQN 可以解决 Cart-Pole 等标准基准测试，并且具有更稳定和更高的平均分数。

AI.tldr: 本文提出量子深度循环强化学习方法，通过构建基于量子长短期记忆网络（QLSTM）的量子强化学习代理，解决部分可观测环境中的量子强化学习问题。
AI.motivation: 量子强化学习在部分可观测环境中的训练仍面临挑战，经典深度强化学习方法在此类任务中存在局限性，需探索量子机器学习新范式。
AI.method: 设计量子循环神经网络（QRNN）作为量子强化学习代理核心，采用量子长短期记忆网络（QLSTM）架构，并结合深度Q学习算法进行模型训练。
AI.result: 数值模拟表明，QLSTM-DRQN在Cart-Pole等基准测试中相较经典DRQN展现出更稳定的训练过程和更高的平均得分。
AI.conclusion: 基于QLSTM的量子深度强化学习方法在部分可观测环境中优于经典方法，验证了量子机器学习在强化学习领域的潜力与优势。

authors_text: Wei Huang,Michelangelo Valsecchi,Michael Multerer
title: Anisotropic multiresolution analyses for deepfake detection
title_zh: 用于深度伪造检测的各向异性多分辨率分析
abstract: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have paved the path towards entirely new media generation capabilities at the forefront of image, video, and audio synthesis. However, they can also be misused and abused to fabricate elaborate lies, capable of stirring up the public debate. The threat posed by GANs has sparked the need to discern between genuine content and fabricated one. Previous studies have tackled this task by using classical machine learning techniques, such as k-nearest neighbours and eigenfaces, which unfortunately did not prove very effective. Subsequent methods have focused on leveraging on frequency decompositions, i.e., discrete cosine transform, wavelets, and wavelet packets, to preprocess the input features for classifiers. However, existing approaches only rely on isotropic transformations. We argue that, since GANs primarily utilize isotropic convolutions to generate their output, they leave clear traces, their fingerprint, in the coefficient distribution on sub-bands extracted by anisotropic transformations. We employ the fully separable wavelet transform and multiwavelets to obtain the anisotropic features to feed to standard CNN classifiers. Lastly, we find the fully separable transform capable of improving the state-of-the-art.
summary: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have paved the path towards entirely new media generation capabilities at the forefront of image, video, and audio synthesis. However, they can also be misused and abused to fabricate elaborate lies, capable of stirring up the public debate. The threat posed by GANs has sparked the need to discern between genuine content and fabricated one. Previous studies have tackled this task by using classical machine learning techniques, such as k-nearest neighbours and eigenfaces, which unfortunately did not prove very effective. Subsequent methods have focused on leveraging on frequency decompositions, i.e., discrete cosine transform, wavelets, and wavelet packets, to preprocess the input features for classifiers. However, existing approaches only rely on isotropic transformations. We argue that, since GANs primarily utilize isotropic convolutions to generate their output, they leave clear traces, their fingerprint, in the coefficient distribution on sub-bands extracted by anisotropic transformations. We employ the fully separable wavelet transform and multiwavelets to obtain the anisotropic features to feed to standard CNN classifiers. Lastly, we find the fully separable transform capable of improving the state-of-the-art.
abstract_zh: 生成对抗网络 (GAN) 为图像、视频和音频合成领域的全新媒体生成能力铺平了道路。然而，它们也可能被误用和滥用来编造精心设计的谎言，从而引发公众辩论。 GAN 带来的威胁引发了辨别真实内容和捏造内容的需要。先前的研究通过使用经典的机器学习技术（例如 k 最近邻和特征脸）来解决此任务，但不幸的是，这些技术并没有被证明非常有效。随后的方法侧重于利用频率分解，即离散余弦变换、小波和小波包，来预处理分类器的输入特征。然而，现有方法仅依赖于各向同性变换。我们认为，由于 GAN 主要利用各向同性卷积来生成输出，因此它们在各向异性变换提取的子带的系数分布中留下了清晰的痕迹，即指纹。我们采用完全可分离的小波变换和多小波来获得各向异性特征，以馈送到标准 CNN 分类器。最后，我们发现完全可分离的变换能够提高最先进的水平。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用各向异性多分辨率分析方法检测深度伪造内容，通过全分离小波变换和多小波提取特征以提升检测效果。
AI.motivation: 现有深度伪造检测方法依赖各向同性变换，无法有效捕捉GAN生成内容在各向异性子带中的特征指纹，导致检测性能不足。
AI.method: 采用全分离小波变换和多小波分解提取图像的各向异性特征，将分解后的子带系数输入标准CNN分类器进行训练与检测。
AI.result: 实验表明，全分离小波变换在深度伪造检测任务中优于传统各向同性变换方法，显著提升检测准确率并刷新了当时最先进的结果。
AI.conclusion: 各向异性多分辨率分析能有效捕捉GAN生成内容的特征指纹，为深度伪造检测提供了新的技术路径和改进方向。

However, they can also be misused and abused to fabricate elaborate lies, capable of stirring up the public debate. The threat posed by GANs has sparked the need to discern between genuine content and fabricated one. Previous studies have tackled this task by using classical machine learning techniques, such as k-nearest neighbours and eigenfaces, which unfortunately did not prove very effective. Subsequent methods have focused on leveraging on frequency decompositions, i.e., discrete cosine transform, wavelets, and wavelet packets, to preprocess the input features for classifiers. However, existing approaches only rely on isotropic transformations. We argue that, since GANs primarily utilize isotropic convolutions to generate their output, they leave clear traces, their fingerprint, in the coefficient distribution on sub-bands extracted by anisotropic transformations. We employ the fully separable wavelet transform and multiwavelets to obtain the anisotropic features to feed to standard CNN classifiers. authors_text: Ziren Xiao,Ruxin Xiao,Chang Liu,Honghao Gao,Xiaolong Xu,Shan Luo,Xinheng Wang
title: An Efficient Dynamic Multi-Sources To Single-Destination (DMS-SD) Algorithm In Smart City Navigation Using Adjacent Matrix
title_zh: 智能城市导航中使用邻接矩阵的高效动态多源到单目的地 (DMS-SD) 算法
comment: International Conference On Human-Centered Cognitive Systems (HCCS) 2022
comments: International Conference On Human-Centered Cognitive Systems (HCCS) 2022
abstract: Dijkstra's algorithm is one of the most popular classic path planning algorithms, achieving optimal solutions across a wide range of challenging tasks. However, it only calculates the shortest distance from one vertex to another, which is hard to directly apply to the Dynamic Multi-Sources to Single-Destination (DMS-SD) problem. This paper proposes a modified Dijkstra algorithm to address the DMS-SD problem, where the destination can be dynamically changed. Our method deploys the concept of Adjacent Matrix from Floyd's algorithm and achieves the goal with mathematical calculations. We formally show that all-pairs shortest distance information in Floyd's algorithm is not required in our algorithm. Extensive experiments verify the scalability and optimality of the proposed method.
summary: Dijkstra's algorithm is one of the most popular classic path planning algorithms, achieving optimal solutions across a wide range of challenging tasks. However, it only calculates the shortest distance from one vertex to another, which is hard to directly apply to the Dynamic Multi-Sources to Single-Destination (DMS-SD) problem. This paper proposes a modified Dijkstra algorithm to address the DMS-SD problem, where the destination can be dynamically changed. Our method deploys the concept of Adjacent Matrix from Floyd's algorithm and achieves the goal with mathematical calculations. We formally show that all-pairs shortest distance information in Floyd's algorithm is not required in our algorithm. Extensive experiments verify the scalability and optimality of the proposed method.
abstract_zh: Dijkstra 算法是最流行的经典路径规划算法之一，可在各种具有挑战性的任务中实现最佳解决方案。然而，它只计算从一个顶点到另一个顶点的最短距离，这很难直接应用于动态多源到单目标（DMS-SD）问题。本文提出了一种改进的Dijkstra算法来解决DMS-SD问题，其中目的地可以动态改变。我们的方法利用了Floyd算法中的邻接矩阵的概念，并通过数学计算来实现目标。我们正式证明，我们的算法不需要弗洛伊德算法中的所有对最短距离信息。大量的实验验证了所提出方法的可扩展性和最优性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于邻接矩阵的改进Dijkstra算法，用于解决智能城市导航中的动态多源单汇（DMS-SD）路径规划问题。
AI.motivation: 传统Dijkstra算法仅适用于单源单汇场景，难以直接处理动态多源单汇的复杂需求，而Floyd算法虽能计算全最短路径但效率较低。
AI.method: 结合Floyd算法的邻接矩阵思想，改进Dijkstra算法，通过数学计算动态更新多源到单汇的最短路径，无需预计算全最短路径信息。
AI.result: 实验验证了该方法在可扩展性和路径最优性方面的优势，能够高效适应动态变化的多源单汇导航需求。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法在保持最优性的同时提升了动态多源单汇场景下的计算效率，适用于智能城市复杂路网的实时导航应用。

authors_text: Mohamed Ashraf Abdelsalam,Zhan Shi,Federico Fancellu,Kalliopi Basioti,Dhaivat J. Bhatt,Vladimir Pavlovic,Afsaneh Fazly
title: Visual Semantic Parsing: From Images to Abstract Meaning Representation
title_zh: 视觉语义解析：从图像到抽象意义表示
abstract: The success of scene graphs for visual scene understanding has brought attention to the benefits of abstracting a visual input (e.g., image) into a structured representation, where entities (people and objects) are nodes connected by edges specifying their relations. Building these representations, however, requires expensive manual annotation in the form of images paired with their scene graphs or frames. These formalisms remain limited in the nature of entities and relations they can capture. In this paper, we propose to leverage a widely-used meaning representation in the field of natural language processing, the Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR), to address these shortcomings. Compared to scene graphs, which largely emphasize spatial relationships, our visual AMR graphs are more linguistically informed, with a focus on higher-level semantic concepts extrapolated from visual input. Moreover, they allow us to generate meta-AMR graphs to unify information contained in multiple image descriptions under one representation. Through extensive experimentation and analysis, we demonstrate that we can re-purpose an existing text-to-AMR parser to parse images into AMRs. Our findings point to important future research directions for improved scene understanding.
summary: The success of scene graphs for visual scene understanding has brought attention to the benefits of abstracting a visual input (e.g., image) into a structured representation, where entities (people and objects) are nodes connected by edges specifying their relations. Building these representations, however, requires expensive manual annotation in the form of images paired with their scene graphs or frames. These formalisms remain limited in the nature of entities and relations they can capture. In this paper, we propose to leverage a widely-used meaning representation in the field of natural language processing, the Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR), to address these shortcomings. Compared to scene graphs, which largely emphasize spatial relationships, our visual AMR graphs are more linguistically informed, with a focus on higher-level semantic concepts extrapolated from visual input. Moreover, they allow us to generate meta-AMR graphs to unify information contained in multiple image descriptions under one representation. Through extensive experimentation and analysis, we demonstrate that we can re-purpose an existing text-to-AMR parser to parse images into AMRs. Our findings point to important future research directions for improved scene understanding.
abstract_zh: 用于视觉场景理解的场景图的成功引起了人们对将视觉输入（例如图像）抽象为结构化表示的好处的关注，其中实体（人和物体）是通过指定其关系的边连接的节点。然而，构建这些表示需要以与其场景图或帧配对的图像形式进行昂贵的手动注释。这些形式主义在它们可以捕获的实体和关系的性质方面仍然有限。在本文中，我们建议利用自然语言处理领域广泛使用的含义表示，即抽象含义表示（AMR）来解决这些缺点。与主要强调空间关系的场景图相比，我们的视觉 AMR 图在语言上更具信息性，重点关注从视觉输入推断出的更高级别的语义概念。此外，它们允许我们生成元 AMR 图，以将多个图像描述中包含的信息统一在一种表示下。通过广泛的实验和分析，我们证明我们可以重新利用现有的文本到 AMR 解析器，将图像解析为 AMR。我们的研究结果指出了改善场景理解的重要未来研究方向。

AI.tldr: 本文提出将图像解析为抽象意义表示（AMR）的视觉语义解析方法，以提升视觉场景理解的语义表达能力。
AI.motivation: 场景图虽能捕捉空间关系，但依赖昂贵的人工标注且语义表达有限，而AMR作为自然语言处理中的语义表示框架，能更高效地捕捉高层语义概念。
AI.method: 通过复用文本到AMR的解析器，将图像转化为视觉AMR图，并构建元AMR图整合多描述信息。
AI.result: 实验验证了图像到AMR解析的可行性，证明该方法能有效生成高层语义表示并统一多源描述信息。
AI.conclusion: 视觉AMR解析为场景理解提供了新方向，未来需进一步优化解析器并探索更丰富的语义表示形式。
