search_text: SCP-GAN: Self-Correcting Discriminator Optimization for Training Consistency Preserving Metric GAN on Speech Enhancement Tasks SCP-GAN：用于语音增强任务的训练一致性保持指标 GAN 的自校正判别器优化 In recent years, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have produced significantly improved results in speech enhancement (SE) tasks. They are difficult to train, however. In this work, we introduce several improvements to the GAN training schemes, which can be applied to most GAN-based SE models. We propose using consistency loss functions, which target the inconsistency in time and time-frequency domains caused by Fourier and Inverse Fourier Transforms. We also present self-correcting optimization for training a GAN discriminator on SE tasks, which helps avoid "harmful" training directions for parts of the discriminator loss function. We have tested our proposed methods on several state-of-the-art GAN-based SE models and obtained consistent improvements, including new state-of-the-art results for the Voice Bank+DEMAND dataset. 近年来，生成对抗网络（GAN）在语音增强（SE）任务中取得了显着改善的结果。然而，它们很难训练。在这项工作中，我们介绍了对 GAN 训练方案的几项改进，这些改进可以应用于大多数基于 GAN 的 SE 模型。我们建议使用一致性损失函数，该函数针对由傅里叶变换和傅里叶逆变换引起的时域和时频域的不一致。我们还提出了在 SE 任务上训练 GAN 判别器的自我校正优化，这有助于避免判别器损失函数部分的“有害”训练方向。我们已经在几种最先进的基于 GAN 的 SE 模型上测试了我们提出的方法，并获得了一致的改进，包括 Voice Bank+DEMAND 数据集的新的最先进的结果。 提出SCP-GAN方法，通过改进GAN训练方案解决语音增强任务中的训练困难问题 Vasily Zadorozhnyy,Qiang Ye,Kazuhito Koishida cs.SD,cs.LG,eess.AS

authors_text: Onkar Susladkar,Dhruv Makwana,Gayatri Deshmukh,Sparsh Mittal,Sai Chandra Teja R,Rekha Singhal
title: TPFNet: A Novel Text In-painting Transformer for Text Removal
title_zh: TPFNet：一种用于文本删除的新型文本绘制转换器
abstract: Text erasure from an image is helpful for various tasks such as image editing and privacy preservation. In this paper, we present TPFNet, a novel one-stage (end-toend) network for text removal from images. Our network has two parts: feature synthesis and image generation. Since noise can be more effectively removed from low-resolution images, part 1 operates on low-resolution images. The output of part 1 is a low-resolution text-free image. Part 2 uses the features learned in part 1 to predict a high-resolution text-free image. In part 1, we use "pyramidal vision transformer" (PVT) as the encoder. Further, we use a novel multi-headed decoder that generates a high-pass filtered image and a segmentation map, in addition to a text-free image. The segmentation branch helps locate the text precisely, and the high-pass branch helps in learning the image structure. To precisely locate the text, TPFNet employs an adversarial loss that is conditional on the segmentation map rather than the input image. On Oxford, SCUT, and SCUT-EnsText datasets, our network outperforms recently proposed networks on nearly all the metrics. For example, on SCUT-EnsText dataset, TPFNet has a PSNR (higher is better) of 39.0 and text-detection precision (lower is better) of 21.1, compared to the best previous technique, which has a PSNR of 32.3 and precision of 53.2. The source code can be obtained from https://github.com/CandleLabAI/TPFNet
summary: Text erasure from an image is helpful for various tasks such as image editing and privacy preservation. In this paper, we present TPFNet, a novel one-stage (end-toend) network for text removal from images. Our network has two parts: feature synthesis and image generation. Since noise can be more effectively removed from low-resolution images, part 1 operates on low-resolution images. The output of part 1 is a low-resolution text-free image. Part 2 uses the features learned in part 1 to predict a high-resolution text-free image. In part 1, we use "pyramidal vision transformer" (PVT) as the encoder. Further, we use a novel multi-headed decoder that generates a high-pass filtered image and a segmentation map, in addition to a text-free image. The segmentation branch helps locate the text precisely, and the high-pass branch helps in learning the image structure. To precisely locate the text, TPFNet employs an adversarial loss that is conditional on the segmentation map rather than the input image. On Oxford, SCUT, and SCUT-EnsText datasets, our network outperforms recently proposed networks on nearly all the metrics. For example, on SCUT-EnsText dataset, TPFNet has a PSNR (higher is better) of 39.0 and text-detection precision (lower is better) of 21.1, compared to the best previous technique, which has a PSNR of 32.3 and precision of 53.2. The source code can be obtained from https://github.com/CandleLabAI/TPFNet
abstract_zh: 从图像中删除文本有助于执行图像编辑和隐私保护等各种任务。在本文中，我们提出了 TPFNet，这是一种新颖的单阶段（端到端）网络，用于从图像中删除文本。我们的网络有两部分：特征合成和图像生成。由于可以更有效地从低分辨率图像中去除噪声，因此第 1 部分对低分辨率图像进行操作。第 1 部分的输出是低分辨率的无文本图像。第 2 部分使用第 1 部分中学到的特征来预测高分辨率的无文本图像。在第 1 部分中，我们使用“金字塔视觉变换器”(PVT) 作为编码器。此外，我们使用一种新颖的多头解码器，除了无文本图像之外，还生成高通滤波图像和分割图。分割分支有助于精确定位文本，高通分支有助于学习图像结构。为了精确定位文本，TPFNet 采用对抗性损失，该损失以分割图而不是输入图像为条件。在 Oxford、SCUT 和 SCUT-EnsText 数据集上，我们的网络在几乎所有指标上都优于最近提出的网络。例如，在 SCUT-EnsText 数据集上，TPFNet 的 PSNR（越高越好）为 39.0，文本检测精度（越低越好）为 21.1，而之前最好的技术的 PSNR 为 32.3，精度为 53.2。源代码可以从https://github.com/CandleLabAI/TPFNet获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出TPFNet，一种用于图像中文字去除的端到端网络，旨在提升图像编辑和隐私保护任务中的文字擦除效果。
AI.motivation: 现有文字去除方法存在效率低或效果不佳的问题，需设计一种能精准定位文字并生成高质量图像的新型网络架构。
AI.method: TPFNet分为两阶段：低分辨率图像特征合成（采用PVT编码器）和高分辨率图像生成（多头解码器生成高通滤波图像、分割图及无文字图像），并通过基于分割图的对抗损失优化文本定位。
AI.result: 在Oxford、SCUT和SCUT-EnsText数据集上，TPFNet在PSNR（如SCUT-EnsText达39.0）和文本检测精度（21.1）等指标上优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: TPFNet通过分阶段处理和多任务学习显著提升了文字去除效果，验证了其在图像编辑和隐私保护中的有效性。

authors_text: Liuchun Yuan,Zehao Huang,Naiyan Wang
title: PredNAS: A Universal and Sample Efficient Neural Architecture Search Framework
title_zh: PredNAS：通用且样本高效的神经架构搜索框架
abstract: In this paper, we present a general and effective framework for Neural Architecture Search (NAS), named PredNAS. The motivation is that given a differentiable performance estimation function, we can directly optimize the architecture towards higher performance by simple gradient ascent. Specifically, we adopt a neural predictor as the performance predictor. Surprisingly, PredNAS can achieve state-of-the-art performances on NAS benchmarks with only a few training samples (less than 100). To validate the universality of our method, we also apply our method on large-scale tasks and compare our method with RegNet on ImageNet and YOLOX on MSCOCO. The results demonstrate that our PredNAS can explore novel architectures with competitive performances under specific computational complexity constraints.
summary: In this paper, we present a general and effective framework for Neural Architecture Search (NAS), named PredNAS. The motivation is that given a differentiable performance estimation function, we can directly optimize the architecture towards higher performance by simple gradient ascent. Specifically, we adopt a neural predictor as the performance predictor. Surprisingly, PredNAS can achieve state-of-the-art performances on NAS benchmarks with only a few training samples (less than 100). To validate the universality of our method, we also apply our method on large-scale tasks and compare our method with RegNet on ImageNet and YOLOX on MSCOCO. The results demonstrate that our PredNAS can explore novel architectures with competitive performances under specific computational complexity constraints.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一个通用且有效的神经架构搜索（NAS）框架，名为 PredNAS。动机是，给定可微分的性能估计函数，我们可以通过简单的梯度上升直接优化架构以获得更高的性能。具体来说，我们采用神经预测器作为性能预测器。令人惊讶的是，PredNAS 只需少量训练样本（少于 100 个）即可在 NAS 基准测试上实现最先进的性能。为了验证我们的方法的普适性，我们还将我们的方法应用于大规模任务，并将我们的方法与 ImageNet 上的 RegNet 和 MSCOCO 上的 YOLOX 进行比较。结果表明，我们的 PredNAS 可以在特定的计算复杂度限制下探索具有竞争性能的新颖架构。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通用且样本高效的神经网络架构搜索框架PredNAS，通过性能预测器直接优化架构以实现高精度。
AI.motivation: 现有NAS方法依赖大量计算资源或样本，而PredNAS通过可微性能估计函数和梯度上升，以少量样本实现高效优化。
AI.method: 采用神经预测器估计模型性能，结合梯度上升直接优化架构参数，并在多个基准任务上验证方法普适性。
AI.result: 在NAS基准测试中仅用不足100个样本即达SOTA性能，且在ImageNet和MSCOCO等大规模任务上表现优于RegNet和YOLOX。
AI.conclusion: PredNAS通过性能预测与梯度优化的结合，在样本效率与架构性能间取得平衡，具有广泛适用性。

ai_content_json.result: 在NAS基准测试中仅用不足100个样本即达SOTA性能，且在ImageNet和MSCOCO等大规模任务上表现优于RegNet和YOLOX。

tldr: 本文提出一种通用且样本高效的神经网络架构搜索框架PredNAS，通过性能预测器直接优化架构以实现高精度。
motivation: 现有NAS方法依赖大量计算资源或样本，而PredNAS通过可微性能估计函数和梯度上升，以少量样本实现高效优化。

search_text: PredNAS: A Universal and Sample Efficient Neural Architecture Search Framework PredNAS：通用且样本高效的神经架构搜索框架 In this paper, we present a general and effective framework for Neural Architecture Search (NAS), named PredNAS. The motivation is that given a differentiable performance estimation function, we can directly optimize the architecture towards higher performance by simple gradient ascent. Specifically, we adopt a neural predictor as the performance predictor. Surprisingly, PredNAS can achieve state-of-the-art performances on NAS benchmarks with only a few training samples (less than 100). To validate the universality of our method, we also apply our method on large-scale tasks and compare our method with RegNet on ImageNet and YOLOX on MSCOCO. The results demonstrate that our PredNAS can explore novel architectures with competitive performances under specific computational complexity constraints. 在本文中，我们提出了一个通用且有效的神经架构搜索（NAS）框架，名为 PredNAS。动机是，给定可微分的性能估计函数，我们可以通过简单的梯度上升直接优化架构以获得更高的性能。具体来说，我们采用神经预测器作为性能预测器。令人惊讶的是，PredNAS 只需少量训练样本（少于 100 个）即可在 NAS 基准测试上实现最先进的性能。为了验证我们的方法的普适性，我们还将我们的方法应用于大规模任务，并将我们的方法与 ImageNet 上的 RegNet 和 MSCOCO 上的 YOLOX 进行比较。结果表明，我们的 PredNAS 可以在特定的计算复杂度限制下探索具有竞争性能的新颖架构。 本文提出一种通用且样本高效的神经网络架构搜索框架PredNAS，通过性能预测器直接优化架构以实现高精度。 Liuchun Yuan,Zehao Huang,Naiyan Wang cs.CV

authors_text: Shichao Dong,Jin Wang,Renhe Ji,Jiajun Liang,Haoqiang Fan,Zheng Ge
title: Implicit Identity Leakage: The Stumbling Block to Improving Deepfake Detection Generalization
title_zh: 隐式身份泄露：提高 Deepfake 检测泛化能力的绊脚石
abstract: In this paper, we analyse the generalization ability of binary classifiers for the task of deepfake detection. We find that the stumbling block to their generalization is caused by the unexpected learned identity representation on images. Termed as the Implicit Identity Leakage, this phenomenon has been qualitatively and quantitatively verified among various DNNs. Furthermore, based on such understanding, we propose a simple yet effective method named the ID-unaware Deepfake Detection Model to reduce the influence of this phenomenon. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art in both in-dataset and cross-dataset evaluation. The code is available at https://github.com/megvii-research/CADDM.
summary: In this paper, we analyse the generalization ability of binary classifiers for the task of deepfake detection. We find that the stumbling block to their generalization is caused by the unexpected learned identity representation on images. Termed as the Implicit Identity Leakage, this phenomenon has been qualitatively and quantitatively verified among various DNNs. Furthermore, based on such understanding, we propose a simple yet effective method named the ID-unaware Deepfake Detection Model to reduce the influence of this phenomenon. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art in both in-dataset and cross-dataset evaluation. The code is available at https://github.com/megvii-research/CADDM.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们分析了二元分类器对于深度伪造检测任务的泛化能力。我们发现，它们泛化的绊脚石是由图像上意外学习到的身份表示引起的。这种现象被称为隐式身份泄露，已经在各种 DNN 中得到了定性和定量的验证。此外，基于这样的理解，我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法，称为 ID-unaware Deepfake 检测模型，以减少这种现象的影响。大量的实验结果表明，我们的方法在数据集内和跨数据集评估方面均优于最先进的方法。代码可在 https://github.com/megvii-research/CADDM 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了深度伪造检测中二分类器的泛化能力问题，提出隐式身份泄露是主要障碍，并设计了ID-unaware检测模型以提升跨数据集性能。
AI.motivation: 现有深度伪造检测模型在跨数据集测试时泛化能力不足，身份信息的隐式表征干扰了模型对伪造特征的学习。
AI.method: 提出ID-unaware Deepfake Detection Model，通过抑制图像中的身份相关特征表示，减少隐式身份泄露对模型泛化的影响。
AI.result: 在in-dataset和cross-dataset实验中均优于现有最先进方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 隐式身份泄露是制约检测模型泛化的核心因素，所提方法通过解耦身份信息有效提升了检测性能。
